name: Gut inflammation memory recorder (tetrathionate)
shortDescription: Records a transient gut-inflammation episode and remembers it. A recombinase memory switch, triggered by the tetrathionate sensor, flips a DNA state that persists after the signal is gone — so a single exposure is still detectable days later in stool.

input.detects: A transient tetrathionate / inflammation episode that may not coincide with sampling
input.operatingRange: Records exposure above the sensor's threshold

sensing.sensorMechanism: The TtrS/TtrR tetrathionate sensor drives a recombinase (integrase) that flips a DNA register; the flipped state is heritable and stable without further signal.
sensing.signalFlow: Tetrathionate -> TtrS/TtrR expresses a recombinase -> the recombinase inverts a DNA segment (set the memory bit) -> the flipped state switches a reporter permanently ON. CRISPRi can gate or reset the register. The record persists after the signal clears.

parts.name: TtrS/TtrR sensor
parts.source: Daeffler/Riglar et al. (tetrathionate sensor in Nissle)
parts.notes: Validated in vivo; triggers the recombinase.

parts.name: Recombinase / integrase (e.g. Bxb1)
parts.notes: Flips a DNA register to store the memory bit.

parts.name: sfGFP reporter
parts.notes: Switched permanently ON by the flipped register; recoverable from stool.

output.result: Cells stay fluorescent after a past inflammation episode, even once tetrathionate is gone.

performance.notes: Recombinase memory + the tetrathionate sensor are each validated in vivo (Riglar et al. 2017); this CRISPRi-gated memory variant is a design template. Captures intermittent inflammation that real-time sensors miss.

safety.biocontainment: Probiotic E. coli Nissle host; add thyA/dapA auxotrophy for gut-restricted containment.
safety.notes: Probiotic chassis with a human-safety record; research / supervised clinical use only.

buildSteps.title: Design the memory register
buildSteps.description: Place a recombinase-invertible register controlling the reporter; design any CRISPRi reset.

buildSteps.title: Assemble units
buildSteps.description: TU1: TtrS/TtrR -> recombinase. TU2: invertible register -> reporter. Optional dCas9 reset arm.

buildSteps.title: Transform E. coli Nissle 1917
buildSteps.description: Select; verify the register flips on tetrathionate and stays flipped.

buildSteps.title: Pulse exposure
buildSteps.description: Apply a transient tetrathionate pulse, then remove it; confirm the memory persists.

buildSteps.title: Recover and read state
buildSteps.description: Recover cells (e.g. from stool) and read the flipped fraction.

provenance.source: Design template: a recombinase memory recorder triggered by the validated tetrathionate sensor, with optional CRISPRi gating
partsSources: Riglar et al. 2017, Nat. Biotechnol. (in-vivo memory sensor)
provenance.license: Parts per their original sources; design template CC BY 4.0
